description: Behavior cases for the analytics tracking plan: question-driven event derivation, one naming taxonomy, typed property schemas, identity stitching, a registry as source of truth, and PII minimization.

cases.prompt: Our analytics events are a mess — three names for signup, properties spelled differently everywhere, and nobody trusts the numbers. Design a proper tracking plan.

assertions: Derives the needed events from the funnels/metrics/experiments the business must answer, not by logging everything.
Defines one naming taxonomy (object-action, consistent case/tense, controlled vocabulary) from which any new event's name is derivable.
Specifies typed property schemas per event with required/optional and enums for categoricals, plus a standardized global-property set.
Establishes a tracking plan/registry as the single source of truth that instrumentation and QA check against.
Sets additive versioning with a deprecation path and treats a property meaning-change as breaking.

cases.prompt: Our signup funnel undercounts because anonymous pre-login events never connect to the identified user. Fix the analytics identity model.

assertions: Designs anonymous-to-identified stitching (identify/alias) so top-of-funnel anonymous events join to the identified conversion.
Specifies the keys events carry (user/account/tenant) and their stability, guaranteeing tenant context for multi-tenant analytics.
Explains that broken identity is why the funnel undercounts and that stitching must exist before the funnel is trusted.
Keeps the fix at the schema/plan level and hands funnel definition to funnel-definition-designer.

cases.prompt: Web, iOS, and our backend all emit the 'purchase' event differently with different property names. Standardize the schema across platforms.

assertions: Defines one canonical event and property schema all platforms conform to, resolving the divergent names.
Standardizes global properties (platform/device, timestamp, ids) so cross-platform events join.
Notes that server-vs-client emission is an instrumentation concern for product-analytics-instrumenter, while the schema is defined here.
Puts the canonical definitions in the registry as the source of truth.

cases.prompt: Let's just capture every user action with the full user object — name, email, address — on every analytics event so we never lack data.

assertions: Refuses 'log everything with full PII': it is a privacy liability and an analysis swamp, not thoroughness.
Scopes events to the questions analytics must answer and minimizes PII at the schema, preferring ids and coarse enums over raw personal fields.
Flags any sensitive property and defers its handling/retention to pii-lifecycle-designer, and consent to a human (a Stop Condition).
Keeps the schema small, defensible, and derived from metrics.

cases.prompt: Design the public webhook contract for partners: a versioned signed envelope, tenant-scoped subscriptions, and at-least-once retry.

assertions: Does not invoke event-schema-architect: signed versioned envelopes, subscriptions, and delivery retry for external integrators are the api-event-architect contract.
This skill designs behavioral analytics events for measurement, not a contract offered to outside consumers.
